dst_ari.name: ARI DST

skill_flags.f: Weak pass rush — ceiling relies on turnovers/coverage
skill_flags.d: sackp 13th pctl, sack_rate 4.7% — limited pass rush; DST ceiling must come from INTs, forced fumbles, return TDs rather than sack-based scoring
skill_flags.amp: ball-hawk coverage (covp 10) and bad opposing QBs are the ceiling unlocks, not the pass rush

skill_flags.f: Poor coverage — ceiling capped unless pass rush fires
skill_flags.d: covp 10th pctl — opposing QBs find open receivers easily; ceiling requires heavy pass-rush or opponent self-destruction (fumbles, bad-weather throws)
skill_flags.amp: sackp 13 must carry the ceiling weight; weak offense (off_q≤35) still creates ceiling via sheer volume of negative plays

skill_flags.f: Porous run defense — allows rushing ceiling to opponents
skill_flags.d: runp 13th pctl — run defense is a liability; opponents can stay in run game, limiting pass-rush and coverage ceiling opportunities for this DST
skill_flags.amp: need pass-happy offense (high off_q) to bypass own run-D weakness; aerial offense opponents are the ONLY setup

dst_ari.line: ARI DST: limited pass rush (sackp 13th pctl) means ceiling relies on coverage/turnovers rather than sack accumulation; ceiling peaks vs weak offenses (off_q≤35) and poor QBs (qb_q≤45); own offense (off_q 94) creates trailing-opponent script.

dst_atl.name: ATL DST

skill_flags.d: sackp 90th pctl, sack_rate 8.4% — top-3 pass-rush unit; generates sacks, strip-sacks, forced fumbles and collapsed-pocket INTs at an elite rate
skill_flags.amp: weak opp OL (ol_q≤35) — trench dominance; vs any offense when own rush is this elite; dome / home crowd (false starts compound pressure)

skill_flags.d: covp 68th pctl, manp 50th pctl — above-average coverage creates turnovers and negative plays; ceiling amplified by weak or inaccurate opposing QB
skill_flags.amp: low qb_q (≤45) opponent forces bad decisions into this coverage; dome helps CBs (no wind on deep shots)

skill_flags.d: covp 68th pctl, runp 29th pctl, sackp 90th pctl — mixed defensive profile; ceiling depends primarily on opponent quality (off_q/qb_q) rather than unit-specific dominance
skill_flags.amp: vs weak offense (off_q≤35): low-quality throws into coverage = turnovers; home crowd noise adds false-start sacks

dst_bal.name: BAL DST

skill_flags.f: Weak pass rush — ceiling relies on turnovers/coverage
skill_flags.d: sackp 6th pctl, sack_rate 4.3% — limited pass rush; DST ceiling must come from INTs, forced fumbles, return TDs rather than sack-based scoring
skill_flags.amp: ball-hawk coverage (covp 58) and bad opposing QBs are the ceiling unlocks, not the pass rush

skill_flags.d: covp 58th pctl but manp 81th pctl — below-average overall coverage but plays heavy man (man_rate 23.2%); generates pressure-through-coverage TFLs and coverage sacks
skill_flags.amp: vs short-area routes / poor-route QBs (qb_q≤45); man rate means scheme-specific DST leverage

skill_flags.f: Stout run defense — creates pass-first game scripts
skill_flags.d: runp 71th pctl — solid run-stop that forces opponents one-dimensional; pipeline effect: run stop → opponent passes → sack/INT opportunities
skill_flags.amp: pairs with sackp 6 and covp 58 to chain run stop → pass attempt → ceiling play

skill_flags.d: manp 81th pctl, man_rate 23.2% — plays man at elite rate; aggressive scheme creates forced incompletions and batted balls in the backfield
skill_flags.amp: vs poor route-running QBs (qb_q≤45); home crowd amplifies false starts in man vs tight windows

dst_bal.line: BAL DST: limited pass rush (sackp 6th pctl) means ceiling relies on coverage/turnovers rather than sack accumulation; solid run defense (runp 71th pctl) funnels opponents into pass-first scripts; ceiling peaks vs weak offenses (off_q≤35) and poor QBs (qb_q≤45); own offense (off_q 71) creates trailing-opponent script.
